1506.05908.title: Piech et al. (2015), "Deep Knowledge Tracing."
1506.05908.description: - **Piech et al. (2015), "Deep Knowledge Tracing."** — https://arxiv.org/abs/1506.05908. The DKT paper that started the deep-learning lineage.

2409.16490.title: Scarlatos & Lan (2024), "Exploring Knowledge Tracing in Tutor-Student Dialogues using LLMs."
2409.16490.description: - **Scarlatos & Lan (2024), "Exploring Knowledge Tracing in Tutor-Student Dialogues using LLMs."** — https://arxiv.org/pdf/2409.16490. *The* most directly relevant paper to your S2 — performs KT on open-ended dialogue turns rather than item responses, exactly the diagnostician's job.

2503.06424.title: Scarlatos, Lee, Liu, Baraniuk, Lan (2025), "Training LLM-based Tutors to Improve Student Learning Outcomes in Dialogues."
2503.06424.description: - **Scarlatos, Lee, Liu, Baraniuk, Lan (2025), "Training LLM-based Tutors to Improve Student Learning Outcomes in Dialogues."** — https://arxiv.org/abs/2503.06424. Uses an LLM-based student model to score candidate tutor utterances and DPO-trains against it. Highly relevant to **[S1, S4]** (the student model literally generates "what-if" predictions).

2502.19915.title: Cen et al. (2025), "LLM-driven Effective Knowledge Tracing by Integrating Dual-channel Difficulty."
2502.19915.description: - **Cen et al. (2025), "LLM-driven Effective Knowledge Tracing by Integrating Dual-channel Difficulty."** — https://arxiv.org/pdf/2502.19915. Lin lab work on LLM-KT.

2505.17705.title: CIKT — Collaborative Iterative Knowledge Tracing (2025).
2505.17705.description: - **CIKT — Collaborative Iterative Knowledge Tracing (2025).** — https://arxiv.org/pdf/2505.17705. Two-LLM framework: an *Analyst* generates structured student profiles, a *Predictor* forecasts performance; trained via Kahneman–Tversky Optimization. Architecturally analogous to your ego/superego split.

2511.02599.title: Next Token Knowledge Tracing (NTKT, 2025).
2511.02599.description: - **Next Token Knowledge Tracing (NTKT, 2025).** — https://arxiv.org/pdf/2511.02599. Reframes KT as next-token prediction on natural-language interaction histories. Directly applicable if the diagnostician maintains a textual learner state.

2506.16982.title: Language Bottleneck Models for Qualitative Knowledge State Modeling (2025).
2506.16982.description: - **Language Bottleneck Models for Qualitative Knowledge State Modeling (2025).** — https://arxiv.org/pdf/2506.16982. Forces the learner state through a natural-language "bottleneck" — interpretable belief-state tracking; arguably the cleanest realization of an externalized learner model.

2602.22879.title: L-HAKT — LLM-Empowered Knowledge Tracing via Hierarchical Behavior Alignment in Hyperbolic Space (2026).
2602.22879.description: - **L-HAKT — LLM-Empowered Knowledge Tracing via Hierarchical Behavior Alignment in Hyperbolic Space (2026).** — https://arxiv.org/pdf/2602.22879. Hyperbolic embedding of KC trees; useful if you go hierarchical.

2603.04855.title: HACHIMI: Scalable and Controllable Student Persona Generation via Orchestrated Agents (2026).
2603.04855.description: - **HACHIMI: Scalable and Controllable Student Persona Generation via Orchestrated Agents (2026).** — https://arxiv.org/pdf/2603.04855. Theory-aligned student persona generation with population-level distribution control. **[S1]** for trajectory bifurcation.

2601.05473.title: Towards Valid Student Simulation with Large Language Models (2026).
2601.05473.description: - **Towards Valid Student Simulation with Large Language Models (2026).** — https://arxiv.org/html/2601.05473. Validation framework for LLM-simulated students. Critical for S1 since your bifurcations depend on a believable student-model.

2511.06078.title: "Simulating Students with Large Language Models: A Review of Architecture" (2025).
2511.06078.description: - **"Simulating Students with Large Language Models: A Review of Architecture" (2025).** — https://arxiv.org/pdf/2511.06078. Survey of architectures for LLM-based simulated students.

2504.06460.title: Can LLMs Simulate Personas with Reversed Performance? (2025).
2504.06460.description: - **Can LLMs Simulate Personas with Reversed Performance? (2025).** — https://arxiv.org/html/2504.06460. LLMs systematically struggle to simulate low-performing students; vital caveat for S1 trajectory generation.

2105.15106.title: Shen et al. (2024), "A Survey of Knowledge Tracing: Models, Variants, and Applications."
2105.15106.description: - **Shen et al. (2024), "A Survey of Knowledge Tracing: Models, Variants, and Applications."** — https://arxiv.org/html/2105.15106v4. Maintained survey covering BKT → DKT → DKVMN → SAKT → SAINT/SAINT+ → AKT → LLM-KT.

2007.12324.title: AKT — Context-Aware Attentive Knowledge Tracing (Ghosh, Heffernan, Lan 2020).
2007.12324.description: - **AKT — Context-Aware Attentive Knowledge Tracing (Ghosh, Heffernan, Lan 2020).** — https://arxiv.org/pdf/2007.12324. Monotonic attention + Rasch embeddings. Still the most cited modern baseline.

2010.12042.title: SAINT+ (Shin et al. 2020).
2010.12042.description: - **SAINT+ (Shin et al. 2020).** — https://arxiv.org/pdf/2010.12042. Transformer encoder–decoder on EdNet; SOTA for years.

2501.05605.title: Domain-Knowledge-Informed Attention KT (2025).
2501.05605.description: - **Domain-Knowledge-Informed Attention KT (2025).** — https://arxiv.org/html/2501.05605v1. XES3G5M benchmark with auxiliary information.

2105.00385.title: pyBKT
2105.00385.description: - **pyBKT** — https://github.com/CAHLR/pyBKT. Pardos lab's Python BKT library with EM fitting, cross-validation, common BKT extensions. Paper: https://arxiv.org/abs/2105.00385. Tutorial: https://ialt.education.ufl.edu/2024/05/06/an-introduction-to-bayesian-knowledge-tracing-with-pybkt/

1912.03072.title: EdNet (Choi et al. 2019).
1912.03072.description: - **EdNet (Choi et al. 2019).** — https://arxiv.org/abs/1912.03072 / https://github.com/riiid/ednet. 131M interactions from Santa; the largest KT dataset.

2409.10244.title: ES-KT-24
2409.10244.description: - **ES-KT-24** — https://arxiv.org/pdf/2409.10244. Multimodal KT with educational-game video + LLM-synthesized text.

2402.01580.title: GAIED workshop series (NeurIPS 2023 → IJCAI 2024 tutorial → ongoing).
2402.01580.description: - **GAIED workshop series (NeurIPS 2023 → IJCAI 2024 tutorial → ongoing).** Survey paper: https://arxiv.org/abs/2402.01580. Tutorial: https://gaied.org/ijcai2024/.

2602.19303.section: Intelligent Tutoring Systems Foundations (primary: S5; secondary: S2, S3) — light coverage
2602.19303.title: The Path to Conversational AI Tutors (Vanacore, Closser, Baker, Roschelle, 2026).
2602.19303.description: - **The Path to Conversational AI Tutors (Vanacore, Closser, Baker, Roschelle, 2026).** — https://arxiv.org/pdf/2602.19303. Maps classical ITS pedagogical primitives onto LLM tutoring; the cleanest bridge document I've seen.

2508.01503.section: Intelligent Tutoring Systems Foundations (primary: S5; secondary: S2, S3) — light coverage
2508.01503.title: A Theory of Adaptive Scaffolding for LLM-Based Pedagogical Agents (2025).
2508.01503.description: - **A Theory of Adaptive Scaffolding for LLM-Based Pedagogical Agents (2025).** — https://arxiv.org/pdf/2508.01503. Synthesizes KLI, ZPD, and ECD with LLM-agent design. **[S5]** anchor paper.

2411.17924.section: Intelligent Tutoring Systems Foundations (primary: S5; secondary: S2, S3) — light coverage
2411.17924.title: AI2T: Building Trustable AI Tutors by Interactively Teaching a Self-Aware Learning Agent (Weitekamp, Harpstead, Koedinger 2024).
2411.17924.description: - **AI2T: Building Trustable AI Tutors by Interactively Teaching a Self-Aware Learning Agent (Weitekamp, Harpstead, Koedinger 2024).** — https://arxiv.org/pdf/2411.17924. CMU lineage; modern Cognitive Tutor.

2603.05778.section: Dialogue Act Classification & Pedagogical Move Taxonomies
2603.05778.title: Tutor Move Taxonomy (Zhou, Vanacore, Thompson, St John, Kizilcec, 2026).
2603.05778.description: - **Tutor Move Taxonomy (Zhou, Vanacore, Thompson, St John, Kizilcec, 2026).** — https://arxiv.org/pdf/2603.05778. *Most directly useful for S3*: a hybrid deductive-inductive taxonomy from the National Tutoring Observatory (Cornell), with four top-level categories and learning-support moves on a spectrum from elicit→explain. This is essentially the codebook you should adopt or adapt.

2406.03486.section: Dialogue Act Classification & Pedagogical Move Taxonomies
2406.03486.title: BIPED: Bilingual Pedagogically-informed Tutoring Dataset (2024).
2406.03486.description: - **BIPED: Bilingual Pedagogically-informed Tutoring Dataset (2024).** — https://arxiv.org/pdf/2406.03486. 34 tutor acts × 9 student acts; "select-act-then-generate" CITS design pattern relevant to **[S3, S2]**.

2509.09125.section: Dialogue Act Classification & Pedagogical Move Taxonomies
2509.09125.title: He & Xu (2024), "Automated Classification of Tutors' Dialogue Acts Using Generative AI: A Case Study Using the CIMA Corpus."
2509.09125.description: - **He & Xu (2024), "Automated Classification of Tutors' Dialogue Acts Using Generative AI: A Case Study Using the CIMA Corpus."** — https://arxiv.org/pdf/2509.09125. GPT-3.5/GPT-4 vs. manual coding on CIMA.

2506.17410.section: Dialogue Act Classification & Pedagogical Move Taxonomies
2506.17410.title: Thomas, Borchers, Lin, et al. (2025), "Leveraging LLMs to Assess Tutor Moves in Real-Life Dialogues: A Feasibility Study."
2506.17410.description: - **Thomas, Borchers, Lin, et al. (2025), "Leveraging LLMs to Assess Tutor Moves in Real-Life Dialogues: A Feasibility Study."** — https://arxiv.org/pdf/2506.17410. CMU + LEVI; benchmarks LLM accuracy at tutor-move classification on real audio transcripts.

2412.13395.section: Dialogue Act Classification & Pedagogical Move Taxonomies
2412.13395.title: Enhancing Talk Moves Analysis in Mathematics Tutoring through Classroom Teaching Discourse (2024).
2412.13395.description: - **Enhancing Talk Moves Analysis in Mathematics Tutoring through Classroom Teaching Discourse (2024).** — https://arxiv.org/html/2412.13395v1. Cross-corpus transfer between TalkMoves and NCTE-annotated tutoring sessions.

2412.09416.section: Dialogue Act Classification & Pedagogical Move Taxonomies
2412.09416.title: Unifying AI Tutor Evaluation: An Evaluation Taxonomy for Pedagogical Ability Assessment of LLM-Powered AI Tutors (Maurya, Petukhova, Kochmar, 2024 → NAACL 2025).
2412.09416.description: - **Unifying AI Tutor Evaluation: An Evaluation Taxonomy for Pedagogical Ability Assessment of LLM-Powered AI Tutors (Maurya, Petukhova, Kochmar, 2024 → NAACL 2025).** — https://arxiv.org/html/2412.09416v1. Eight-dimension pedagogical-ability rubric (Mistake Identification, Mistake Location, Guidance, Actionability, Coherence, Tutor Tone, Humanlikeness, Encouragement).

2204.09652.section: Dialogue Act Classification & Pedagogical Move Taxonomies
2204.09652.title: TalkMoves dataset & application.
2204.09652.description: - **TalkMoves dataset & application.** — https://github.com/SumnerLab/TalkMoves; paper https://arxiv.org/abs/2204.09652. 567 K-12 math lesson transcripts annotated for ten discursive moves + Switchboard-DA labels.

2508.21204.title: Figueiredo (2025), "Fuzzy, Symbolic, and Contextual: Enhancing LLM Instruction via Cognitive Scaffolding."
2508.21204.description: - **Figueiredo (2025), "Fuzzy, Symbolic, and Contextual: Enhancing LLM Instruction via Cognitive Scaffolding."** — https://arxiv.org/pdf/2508.21204. Three-layer prompt-level scaffold with short-term schema; explicitly Vygotskian.

2508.06754.title: "A Fuzzy Logic Prompting Framework for Large Language Models in Adaptive and Uncertain Tasks" (2025).
2508.06754.description: - **"A Fuzzy Logic Prompting Framework for Large Language Models in Adaptive and Uncertain Tasks" (2025).** — https://arxiv.org/html/2508.06754. Boundary-prompt + control-schema design pattern.

2506.19484.title: "arXiv:2506.19484 — LLMs and Vygotsky" (2025).
2506.19484.description: - **"arXiv:2506.19484 — LLMs and Vygotsky" (2025).** — https://arxiv.org/pdf/2506.19484. Maps LLM behaviors onto Vygotskian theory point-by-point. Useful for the discussion section of your follow-up.

2210.03629.title: Yao et al. (2023), "ReAct: Synergizing Reasoning and Acting in Language Models."
2210.03629.description: - **Yao et al. (2023), "ReAct: Synergizing Reasoning and Acting in Language Models."** — https://arxiv.org/abs/2210.03629. The canonical reason-act loop.

2303.11366.title: Shinn et al. (2023), "Reflexion: Language Agents with Verbal Reinforcement Learning."
2303.11366.description: - **Shinn et al. (2023), "Reflexion: Language Agents with Verbal Reinforcement Learning."** — https://arxiv.org/abs/2303.11366. Verbal self-feedback; relevant to a critic/diagnostician role.

2305.16291.title: Wang et al. (2023), "Voyager: An Open-Ended Embodied Agent with LLMs."
2305.16291.description: - **Wang et al. (2023), "Voyager: An Open-Ended Embodied Agent with LLMs."** — https://arxiv.org/abs/2305.16291. Skill-library + automatic curriculum; conceptually relevant for S6 (skills accumulating across sessions).

2304.03442.title: Park et al. (2023), "Generative Agents: Interactive Simulacra of Human Behavior."
2304.03442.description: - **Park et al. (2023), "Generative Agents: Interactive Simulacra of Human Behavior."** — https://arxiv.org/abs/2304.03442 / https://dl.acm.org/doi/fullHtml/10.1145/3586183.3606763. Observation-reflection-planning memory architecture; the persistent-memory paradigm for S6.

2601.12560.title: "Agentic AI: Architectures, Taxonomies, and Evaluation of LLM Agents" (2026).
2601.12560.description: - **"Agentic AI: Architectures, Taxonomies, and Evaluation of LLM Agents" (2026).** — https://arxiv.org/html/2601.12560v1.

2601.01743.title: "AI Agent Systems: Architectures, Applications, and Evaluation" (2026).
2601.01743.description: - **"AI Agent Systems: Architectures, Applications, and Evaluation" (2026).** — https://arxiv.org/html/2601.01743v1.

2502.11008.title: CounterBench (Tang et al. 2025).
2502.11008.description: - **CounterBench (Tang et al. 2025).** — https://arxiv.org/pdf/2502.11008. Benchmark for counterfactual reasoning in LLMs; introduces the "CoIn" paradigm.

2410.21131.title: "Towards Unifying Evaluation of Counterfactual Explanations: Leveraging LLMs for Human-Centric Assessments" (2024).
2410.21131.description: - **"Towards Unifying Evaluation of Counterfactual Explanations: Leveraging LLMs for Human-Centric Assessments" (2024).** — https://arxiv.org/pdf/2410.21131. LLM as benchmarker of CF explanations.

2505.17801.title: AXIS (2025), "Integrating Counterfactual Simulations with Language Models for Explaining Multi-Agent Behaviour."
2505.17801.description: - **AXIS (2025), "Integrating Counterfactual Simulations with Language Models for Explaining Multi-Agent Behaviour."** — https://arxiv.org/html/2505.17801. Closest existing work to your bifurcation idea, in the multi-agent-RL setting.

2403.03956.title: Backtracing (Wang, Wirawarn, Khattab, Goodman, Demszky, EACL Findings 2024).
2403.03956.description: - **Backtracing (Wang, Wirawarn, Khattab, Goodman, Demszky, EACL Findings 2024).** — https://arxiv.org/abs/2403.03956. "Retrieve the cause of the query": given a student turn, find the antecedent tutor turn that produced the misconception. Methodologically adjacent to bifurcation.

2410.03017.title: Tutor CoPilot (Wang, Ribeiro, Robinson, Loeb, Demszky 2024).
2410.03017.description: - **Tutor CoPilot (Wang, Ribeiro, Robinson, Loeb, Demszky 2024).** — https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.03017. Real-time AI suggestions to live human tutors; the deployment study includes natural counterfactual variation.

2306.05685.title: Zheng et al. (2023), "Judging LLM-as-a-Judge with MT-Bench and Chatbot Arena."
2306.05685.description: - **Zheng et al. (2023), "Judging LLM-as-a-Judge with MT-Bench and Chatbot Arena."** — https://arxiv.org/abs/2306.05685. Required reading; introduces pairwise vs. single-answer protocols and quantifies position/verbosity biases.

2403.04132.title: LMSYS Chatbot Arena (Chiang et al., 2024).
2403.04132.description: - **LMSYS Chatbot Arena (Chiang et al., 2024).** — https://arxiv.org/abs/2403.04132. Crowd-sourced pairwise preference ranking infrastructure.

2406.07791.title: Shi et al. (2024–25), "Judging the Judges: A Systematic Study of Position Bias in LLM-as-a-Judge."
2406.07791.description: - **Shi et al. (2024–25), "Judging the Judges: A Systematic Study of Position Bias in LLM-as-a-Judge."** — https://arxiv.org/abs/2406.07791. Three metrics: repetition stability, position consistency, preference fairness; 150K eval instances. *Critical for S4 design.*

2406.12319.title: "The Comparative Trap: Pairwise Comparisons Amplifies Biased Preferences of LLM Evaluators" (2024).
2406.12319.description: - **"The Comparative Trap: Pairwise Comparisons Amplifies Biased Preferences of LLM Evaluators" (2024).** — https://arxiv.org/pdf/2406.12319. Counterargument to default pairwise; pairwise *amplifies* known biases on adversarial examples.

2403.16950.title: "Aligning with Human Judgement: The Role of Pairwise Preference in LLM Evaluators" (2024).
2403.16950.description: - **"Aligning with Human Judgement: The Role of Pairwise Preference in LLM Evaluators" (2024).** — https://arxiv.org/pdf/2403.16950.

2602.16610.title: "Who can we trust? LLM-as-a-jury for Comparative Assessment" (2026).
2602.16610.description: - **"Who can we trust? LLM-as-a-jury for Comparative Assessment" (2026).** — https://arxiv.org/pdf/2602.16610. Multi-judge jury with reliability weighting; recommended pattern for S4 if budget allows.

2508.09724.title: UDA — Unsupervised Debiasing Alignment for Pair-wise LLM-as-a-Judge (2025).
2508.09724.description: - **UDA — Unsupervised Debiasing Alignment for Pair-wise LLM-as-a-Judge (2025).** — https://arxiv.org/pdf/2508.09724. Dynamic recalibration via judge consensus; a usable methodology for your S4 robustness section.

2509.26072.title: "The Silent Judge: Unacknowledged Shortcut Bias in LLM-as-a-Judge" (NeurIPS 2025 workshop).
2509.26072.description: - **"The Silent Judge: Unacknowledged Shortcut Bias in LLM-as-a-Judge" (NeurIPS 2025 workshop).** — https://arxiv.org/pdf/2509.26072. Recency and provenance bias (EXPERT > HUMAN > LLM > UNKNOWN). Vital — your hold-out optimal-continuation pairs in S4 must avoid these shortcut features.

2602.06625.title: FairJudge: An Adaptive, Debiased, and Consistent LLM-as-a-Judge (2026).
2602.06625.description: - **FairJudge: An Adaptive, Debiased, and Consistent LLM-as-a-Judge (2026).** — https://arxiv.org/pdf/2602.06625. Pointwise–pairwise inconsistency analysis.

2507.10579.title: BEA 2025 Shared Task on Pedagogical Ability Assessment of AI-powered Tutors.
2507.10579.description: - **BEA 2025 Shared Task on Pedagogical Ability Assessment of AI-powered Tutors.** — https://sig-edu.org/sharedtask/2025. Findings paper https://arxiv.org/html/2507.10579v1. *MRBench* benchmark dataset built on MathDial+Bridge; eight pedagogical dimensions. *The* domain-specific judge benchmark — required for S4.

2502.18940.title: MathTutorBench (Macina, Daheim, Hakimi, Kapur, Gurevych, Sachan 2025).
2502.18940.description: - **MathTutorBench (Macina, Daheim, Hakimi, Kapur, Gurevych, Sachan 2025).** — https://arxiv.org/abs/2502.18940. Pairwise expert-vs.-novice teacher response judgment; reward-model-based scoring. https://eth-lre.github.io/mathtutorbench/. **This is essentially S4 already done at corpus scale; build on it.**

2510.23477.title: MMTutorBench (2025).
2510.23477.description: - **MMTutorBench (2025).** — https://arxiv.org/pdf/2510.23477. Multimodal extension; relevant only if you go visual.

2507.22753.title: EduDial
2507.22753.description: - **EduDial** and other 2025 corpora are surveyed in: https://arxiv.org/html/2507.22753v1 ("Opportunities and Challenges of LLMs in Education: An NLP Perspective").

2306.15448.section: Theory of Mind & Learner Modeling in LLMs
2306.15448.title: BigToM (Gandhi, Fränken, Gerstenberg, Goodman, NeurIPS 2023).
2306.15448.description: - **BigToM (Gandhi, Fränken, Gerstenberg, Goodman, NeurIPS 2023).** — https://arxiv.org/abs/2306.15448. Causal-template ToM benchmark.

2310.15421.section: Theory of Mind & Learner Modeling in LLMs
2310.15421.title: FANToM (Kim et al., EMNLP 2023).
2310.15421.description: - **FANToM (Kim et al., EMNLP 2023).** — https://hyunw.kim/fantom/ / https://arxiv.org/abs/2310.15421. Information-asymmetric conversational ToM. *Most relevant for S2 since tutoring is fundamentally information-asymmetric dialogue.*

2402.06044.section: Theory of Mind & Learner Modeling in LLMs
2402.06044.title: OpenToM (Xu et al., 2024).
2402.06044.description: - **OpenToM (Xu et al., 2024).** — https://arxiv.org/abs/2402.06044. Personality- and intention-grounded ToM with both physical and psychological mental states.

2402.15052.section: Theory of Mind & Learner Modeling in LLMs
2402.15052.title: ToMBench (Chen et al., 2024).
2402.15052.description: - **ToMBench (Chen et al., 2024).** — https://arxiv.org/abs/2402.15052. Holistic ToM eval addressing scope/subjectivity issues.

2412.12175.section: Theory of Mind & Learner Modeling in LLMs
2412.12175.title: ExploreToM (Sclar et al., Meta 2024).
2412.12175.description: - **ExploreToM (Sclar et al., Meta 2024).** — https://arxiv.org/abs/2412.12175 / https://github.com/facebookresearch/exploretom. A* search over a domain-specific language to generate adversarial false-belief stories. SOTA LLMs at 0–9% on hard slice. *The single most informative ToM benchmark for diagnostician-agent design — directly stress-tests state tracking.*

2302.08399.section: Theory of Mind & Learner Modeling in LLMs
2302.08399.title: Ullman (2023), "Large language models fail on trivial alterations to theory-of-mind tasks."
2302.08399.description: - **Ullman (2023), "Large language models fail on trivial alterations to theory-of-mind tasks."** — https://arxiv.org/abs/2302.08399. Mandatory caveat.

2507.15788.section: Theory of Mind & Learner Modeling in LLMs
2507.15788.title: "Small LLMs Do Not Learn a Generalizable Theory of Mind via Reinforcement Learning" (2025).
2507.15788.description: - **"Small LLMs Do Not Learn a Generalizable Theory of Mind via Reinforcement Learning" (2025).** — https://arxiv.org/html/2507.15788. RLVR on ToM data fails to generalize. Methodologically informative for S2 — your diagnostician should not rely on small fine-tuned ToM modules.

2310.08560.title: MemGPT (Packer et al., Berkeley Sky Lab, 2023).
2310.08560.description: - **MemGPT (Packer et al., Berkeley Sky Lab, 2023).** — https://arxiv.org/abs/2310.08560 / https://research.memgpt.ai/. OS-inspired hierarchical memory with self-managed paging; the foundation for Letta.

2508.03275.title: LECTOR (2025), "LLM-Enhanced Concept-based Test-Oriented Repetition for Adaptive Spaced Learning."
2508.03275.description: - **LECTOR (2025), "LLM-Enhanced Concept-based Test-Oriented Repetition for Adaptive Spaced Learning."** — https://arxiv.org/abs/2508.03275. LLM-powered semantic-similarity layer over FSRS/SSP-MMC.

2512.12856.title: "Forgetful but Faithful: A Cognitive Memory Architecture for Privacy-Aware Generative Agents" (2025).
2512.12856.description: - **"Forgetful but Faithful: A Cognitive Memory Architecture for Privacy-Aware Generative Agents" (2025).** — https://arxiv.org/html/2512.12856v1.

2603.04740.title: "Memory in the Age of AI Agents" (Dec 2025 survey).
2603.04740.description: - **"Memory in the Age of AI Agents" (Dec 2025 survey).** — A December 2025 large-scale taxonomic survey on three-axis (form/function/dynamics) memory categorization (referenced in https://arxiv.org/html/2603.04740v1).
